name: xsint
description: All-in-one CLI OSINT aggregator that takes an email, phone, username, IP, or address and fans the query out across 67+ services and modules.
bestFor: Quickly pivoting from a single identifier (email/phone/username/IP/address) to account-presence hits, breach exposure, phone metadata, and geocoded addresses in one command.
trustNote: Open-source (GPL v3) Python CLI by GitHub user h1lw; small/new project, verify behavior before relying on results. Some modules require third-party logins/API keys (Google session, GitHub PAT, HIBP, IntelX, Telegram).
notes: Requires Python 3.10+; install via the repo install.sh (Mac/Linux). Input can be type-prefixed (email:, phone:, user:, addr:, ip:). email_enum checks ~67 services across 18 categories; phone_enum checks Amazon and Snapchat presence; instagram module pulls masked recovery email/phone from IG's recovery flow; phone_basic returns carrier/line-type/timezone; osm module geocodes addresses via OpenStreetMap. Optional modules wrap GHunt (Google), GitFive (GitHub), HaveIBeenPwned, IntelX, and a Telegram breach bot (Haxalot) and require their own auth. Supports Tor/Burp proxying for OPSEC. Many modules are ACTIVE (they hit account recovery endpoints), so use a sock/proxy.

name: GHunt
description: Offensive Google framework that resolves a Gmail address or Gaia ID to the public Google profile: name, profile photo, Google Maps reviews/activity, and linked services.
bestFor: Turning a Gmail/Google email into an identity: profile name, photo, public Maps reviews (which can reveal location patterns), and linked Google services.
trustNote: Widely used, well-known OSINT tool by mxrch; integrated into many OSINT suites. AGPL licensed.
notes: Integrated by xsint as the ghunt_lookup module. Requires authenticating with a Google account session (cookies stored locally). Search by email, Gaia ID, YouTube channel, or Google Drive/Docs URL. Public Google Maps reviews tied to the account can reveal a target's frequented locations - high value for locating a person.

name: GitFive
description: OSINT tool to track down GitHub users: resolves email<->GitHub-account, recovers username/name history and variations, dumps SSH keys, and surfaces secondary accounts.
bestFor: Pivoting between an email and a GitHub identity, and uncovering a target's username history, name variations, and secondary/linked accounts from commit metadata.
trustNote: Well-known OSINT tool by mxrch (same author as GHunt); on PyPI and many OSINT tool indexes.
notes: Integrated by xsint as the gitfive_module. Requires a GitHub Personal Access Token. Can extract an email tied to a GitHub account (and vice versa), find name/username variations and likely secondary accounts. Useful when a missing person is technical/has a developer footprint; medium relevance otherwise. Python 3.10+.

name: Have I Been Pwned (HIBP)
description: Breach-notification service that reports which data breaches an email address or phone number appeared in, with breach names and dates.
bestFor: Confirming an email/phone is real and active, and enumerating the breaches it appears in (which can reveal sites/services the person used).
trustNote: Authoritative, widely trusted breach database run by Troy Hunt. Web lookup is free; programmatic email search requires a paid API key, but breach metadata returned is reliable.
notes: Integrated by xsint via the hibp module (xsint --auth hibp <key>). Web UI lookups are free; the email-search API requires a paid subscription key. Breach list shows which services an email registered with, which can suggest additional platforms/usernames to pivot on. Medium relevance: corroborative, not a direct locator.

name: Intelligence X (IntelX)
description: Search engine and data archive for leaks, pastes, darknet, and documents indexed by selectors such as email, domain, IP, and more.
bestFor: Finding leaked credentials, pastes, and documents containing a target email/phone/domain, including content removed from the surface web.
trustNote: Established commercial OSINT search/archive service. Limited free tier and free web search; deeper results and API access are paid.
notes: Integrated by xsint via the intelx module (xsint --auth intelx <key>). Free tier is rate-limited and redacts some results; full leak/paste/document content needs a paid plan. Can surface pastes/leaks mentioning a person, occasionally with additional PII to pivot on.

name: OpenStreetMap Nominatim
description: Free geocoding service from OpenStreetMap that converts a street address into latitude/longitude coordinates and structured location data.
bestFor: Geocoding an address obtained during an investigation into precise coordinates for mapping, distance, and area analysis.
trustNote: Official OpenStreetMap geocoder; free and open. Public endpoint has a strict usage policy (max ~1 req/sec, no heavy bulk use).
notes: Integrated by xsint as the osm module to geocode an input address into coordinates. Respect the Nominatim usage policy (rate-limit, set a User-Agent) or self-host. Useful for placing a last-known address on a map and computing search radii.
